title: Postal Service Clerks
description: Perform any combination of tasks in a United States Postal Service (USPS) post office, such as receive letters and parcels; sell postage and revenue stamps, postal cards, and stamped envelopes; fill out and sell money orders; place mail in pigeon holes of mail rack or in bags; and examine mail for correct postage. Includes postal service clerks employed by USPS contractors.

tasks.text: Weigh letters and parcels, compute mailing costs based on type, weight, and destination, and affix correct postage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check mail to ensure correct postage and that packages and letters are in proper condition for mailing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sort incoming and outgoing mail, according to type and destination, by hand or by operating electronic mail-sorting and scanning devices.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Obtain signatures from recipients of registered or special delivery mail.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer questions regarding mail regulations and procedures, postage rates, and post office boxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transport mail from one work station to another.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sell and collect payment for products such as stamps, prepaid mail envelopes, and money orders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep money drawers in order, and record and balance daily transactions.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Register, certify, and insure letters and parcels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete forms regarding changes of address, or theft or loss of mail, or for special services such as registered or priority mail.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive letters and parcels, and place mail into bags.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Put undelivered parcels away, retrieve them when customers come to claim them, and complete any related documentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Respond to complaints regarding mail theft, delivery problems, and lost or damaged mail, filling out forms and making appropriate referrals for investigation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide assistance to the public in complying with federal regulations of Postal Service and other federal agencies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Rent post office boxes to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide customers with assistance in filing claims for mail theft, or lost or damaged mail.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Feed mail into postage canceling devices or hand stamp mail to cancel postage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order retail items and other supplies for office use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
